item_batch_editor_lessons.conversation_reference: Item batch editor implementation session - comprehensive implementation from planning through debugging async exports
item_batch_editor_lessons.purpose: Capture Item batch editor as the new state-of-the-art reference implementation, synthesizing all lessons learned from Languoid and Collaborator implementations plus new discoveries. This file serves as the comprehensive guide for implementing the final Document batch editor in Stage 1.

executive_summary.state_of_the_art: Item batch editor is now the most complete and sophisticated batch editor implementation, superseding Collaborator batch editor as the reference implementation for future work.
executive_summary.efficiency_gains: Successfully leveraged existing patterns from batch-editor-universal-patterns.json and collaborator_batch_editor_lessons.json, resulting in faster initial implementation compared to Collaborator batch editor development time.
executive_summary.pattern_fidelity_discovery: While leveraging existing patterns improved efficiency, implementation revealed additional high-fidelity pattern-following requirements that weren't initially apparent. These discoveries led to iterative refinements and deeper understanding of what 'following existing patterns' truly requires.

new_complexity_handled.complex_through_models: CollaboratorRole with MultiSelectField roles + boolean citation_author
new_complexity_handled.virtual_computed_fields: primary_title and secondary_title as SerializerMethodField aggregating ItemTitle data
new_complexity_handled.invalid_data_preservation: Preserving user input with id: null for correction instead of silently dropping
new_complexity_handled.61_fields: Largest batch editor to date with most complex field types and relationships

reusable_components_created: CollaboratorRolesCellEditor - handles any through-model with attributes (multiselect + booleans)
TitleWithLanguageCellEditor - handles text + FK selection pattern
Enhanced multiselect with invalid item visualization and handling
Import parsers for complex multi-attribute fields
executive_summary.document_batch_editor_readiness: With Item batch editor complete, all patterns and components are in place for efficient Document batch editor implementation. Document will likely be simpler (fewer complex fields) and can directly reference Item patterns.

pattern_fidelity_lessons.content: Lessons learned about following existing patterns with higher fidelity

lesson_1_redis_caching.what_happened: Initially implemented Item batch editor without Redis caching, assuming it was optional. Hit sessionStorage quota errors with 4,396 items × 61 fields (15.32MB).
lesson_1_redis_caching.what_we_learned: Redis caching is NOT optional for any model with >1000 rows. Must be implemented from the start, not retrofitted.
lesson_1_redis_caching.correct_pattern: Follow InternalCollaboratorViewSet list() method pattern: check cache first, return 202 Accepted for async rebuild if stale, implement warm_item_list_cache Celery task.
lesson_1_redis_caching.for_document_editor: Implement Redis caching in very first iteration, before even testing with real data.

lesson_2_export_filenames.what_happened: Initial implementation used timestamp-based export IDs (YYYY-MM-DD_HHMMSS) in backend, which caused subtle issues with async task execution and filename collisions.
lesson_2_export_filenames.what_we_learned: Separate concerns: Backend needs guaranteed uniqueness (UUID), Frontend needs human-readability (formatted timestamp).
lesson_2_export_filenames.correct_pattern: Backend: export_id = str(uuid.uuid4()). Frontend: Generate timestamp on download in YYYY-MM-DD_HHMMSS format for link.download attribute.
lesson_2_export_filenames.for_document_editor: Use UUID backend + formatted timestamp frontend from the start.

lesson_3_client_side_filtering.what_happened: Export and batch edit initially used paginated items state, resulting in only 25 rows being processed instead of full filtered dataset.
lesson_3_client_side_filtering.what_we_learned: Client-side filtering for exports and batch edits must operate on the FULL cached dataset, not the current pagination page.
lesson_3_client_side_filtering.correct_pattern: Use allCachedItems = await getItems() then apply filters client-side, send resulting IDs to backend.
lesson_3_client_side_filtering.for_document_editor: Always use full cache + client-side filter for exports/batch operations, never use paginated state.

lesson_4_async_export_error_handling.what_happened: Item async export task initially used raise self.retry() pattern, causing silent failures and corrupted 2KB files.
lesson_4_async_export_error_handling.what_we_learned: Task error handling pattern is inconsistent across models. Item works with return {'status': 'error'}, Collaborator uses raise self.retry().
lesson_4_async_export_error_handling.correct_pattern: For new tasks, use return error dict pattern (simpler, more predictable). Keep decorator: @shared_task(bind=True, max_retries=3, default_retry_delay=30).
lesson_4_async_export_error_handling.for_document_editor: Use return {'status': 'error', 'error': str(e)} pattern in async export task.

lesson_5_virtual_field_validation.what_happened: Import validation failed for primary_title and secondary_title with 'Method POST not allowed' errors because backend tried to validate SerializerMethodField.
lesson_5_virtual_field_validation.what_we_learned: Virtual/computed fields (SerializerMethodField) cannot be validated via backend API. Parser must handle validation, backend validation must be skipped.
lesson_5_virtual_field_validation.correct_pattern: Add virtual field names to skipValidationFields array in import hook. Parser errors mark cell as invalid.
lesson_5_virtual_field_validation.for_document_editor: Identify virtual fields early, add to skip list, ensure parsers have comprehensive validation.

lesson_6_sessionids_persistence.what_happened: New rows disappeared on browser refresh (F5) because sessionIds.current was in-memory only.
lesson_6_sessionids_persistence.what_we_learned: Must persist new row IDs to sessionStorage config to survive browser refresh, not just in-page refresh.
lesson_6_sessionids_persistence.correct_pattern: After saving draft rows, update sessionStorage '{model}-batch-config' with new IDs: config.ids = Array.from(sessionIds.current).
lesson_6_sessionids_persistence.for_document_editor: Update sessionStorage config immediately after successful save of new rows.

new_universal_patterns_discovered.content: New patterns discovered during Item implementation that apply to future batch editors

pattern_complex_through_model.problem: Through models that aren't just junction tables but have their own complex attributes (MultiSelectField, booleans, etc.)
pattern_complex_through_model.example: CollaboratorRole: FK to Collaborator + roles (MultiSelectField with 20+ choices) + citation_author (boolean)

implementation_requirements.backend_serializer: Use SerializerMethodField for read (format as objects), handle in create/update methods for write (create/update through model instances)
implementation_requirements.frontend_column_type: Custom cellType (e.g., 'collaborator_roles') with dedicated cell editor component

custom_editor_pattern.component_structure: Search/add collaborators, display as cards, edit attributes per card (dropdown for multiselect, checkbox for boolean)
custom_editor_pattern.state_management: selectedCollaborators array with {id, name, roles[], citation_author} objects
custom_editor_pattern.commit_logic: Compare original vs current (separate valid/invalid), detect ANY changes, commit if changed, cancel if unchanged
custom_editor_pattern.invalid_handling: Show invalid items (id: null) as red cards, block commit if invalid remain, detect invalid removal as change

import_export.import_parsing: Parse name → lookup by full_name, parse roles → map labels to values, parse 'in citation' → boolean
import_export.error_handling: Preserve invalid with id: null, show red cell, allow user correction

pattern_complex_through_model.reusable_for: Any through model with attributes beyond simple junction (e.g., Document-Contributor with contribution_type + primary_flag)

pattern_virtual_aggregated_fields.problem: Fields that don't exist on main model but aggregate data from related through-model (e.g., 'default title' from multiple ItemTitle records)
pattern_virtual_aggregated_fields.example: primary_title and secondary_title: Aggregate ItemTitle where default=True or default=False, format as {title: string, language: {id, name, glottocode}}

implementation_requirements.backend_serializer: SerializerMethodField with get_* method that queries through model, formats response. Handle in update/create with explicit through-model CRUD.
implementation_requirements.frontend_column_type: Custom cellType (e.g., 'title_with_language') with dedicated cell editor
implementation_requirements.validation_strategy: Skip backend validation (add to skipValidationFields), parser validates and formats

backend_update_pattern.in_update_method: Extract *_data from validated_data, query existing through-model instances, create/update/delete as needed
backend_update_pattern.in_create_method: Same pattern - must handle through-model creation for new parent instances
backend_update_pattern.transaction_safety: Wrap in try-except, through-model changes part of parent transaction

custom_editor_pattern.component_structure: TextField for text input + Autocomplete for FK selection (e.g., Language)
custom_editor_pattern.commit_logic: Simple comparison of title and language.id, commit if changed
custom_editor_pattern.keyboard_handling: Enter commits if autocomplete closed, Escape cancels, handle autocomplete open state

import_export.import_parsing: Parse title text, extract language name from parentheses, lookup language by name (must be unique)
import_export.error_handling: Show red cell if language not found or not unique, preserve original input

pattern_virtual_aggregated_fields.reusable_for: Any virtual field that aggregates through-model data (e.g., Document primary_subject from DocumentSubject through model)

pattern_invalid_data_preservation.pattern_name: Invalid Data Preservation for User Correction
pattern_invalid_data_preservation.problem: User imports data with typos or non-existent references. Old approach: silently drop invalid data. User: confused why data disappeared.
pattern_invalid_data_preservation.solution: Preserve invalid data with id: null, visualize as red/invalid, allow user to see what's wrong and fix it

implementation_steps.step_1_parser: When lookup fails (e.g., languoid not found), add to array with id: null and original text: {id: null, name: 'Original Input', glottocode: null}
implementation_steps.step_2_validation: Mark cell as invalid (validationState: 'invalid') if ANY items have id: null
implementation_steps.step_3_visualization: In custom editors, render id: null items with red styling (border, background, text)
implementation_steps.step_4_commit_blocking: Block save if invalid items remain - cell stays red
implementation_steps.step_5_change_detection: Detect removal of invalid items as a change - cell turns yellow when fixed

editor_behavior.multiselect: Show invalid items as red chips, allow deletion, cancel commit if invalid remain
editor_behavior.through_model: Show invalid items as red cards with 'Not found in database' label, same delete/cancel logic
editor_behavior.comparison_logic: Separate valid and invalid items, compare only valid items for equality, flag invalid presence

user_workflow.import_with_typo: User imports 'Engliswwh (stan6593)' → Parser marks invalid → Cell red → Tooltip shows error
user_workflow.user_sees_error: Opens editor → Sees 'Engliswwh' as red chip → Realizes typo
user_workflow.user_corrects: Deletes red chip → Valid items remain → Cell turns yellow (changed) → Can save
user_workflow.alternative: Cancels → Cell stays red → Can fix in spreadsheet and re-import

technical_details.unique_key_strategy: Generate _uniqueKey for all items (valid or invalid) for stable React rendering: 'valid-{id}' or 'invalid-{timestamp}-{index}-{name}'
technical_details.state_management: Track isValid flag per item, filter/separate in commit logic
technical_details.paste_handling: Structured paste preserves id: null items, plain text paste triggers parser

pattern_invalid_data_preservation.reusable_for: Any field with FK lookups or complex validation where user input might be invalid (languoids, collaborators, subjects, genres)
pattern_invalid_data_preservation.anti_pattern: Silently dropping invalid data - user loses work and has no idea why

pattern_multiselect_invalid_enhancement.problem: Standard multiselect editor doesn't distinguish valid vs invalid items from import
pattern_multiselect_invalid_enhancement.solution: Enhance CellEditor.tsx multiselect case to filter, visualize, and handle invalid items

implementation.initialization: Filter out id: null items OR create fake chips with isValid: false flag
implementation.visualization: Conditional Chip styling: red border + background for isValid === false
implementation.commit_logic: Check if ANY invalid items remain → cancel. Check if invalid items removed → commit (change detected).
implementation.comparison: Compare only valid item IDs: originalValidIds vs currentValidIds

pattern_multiselect_invalid_enhancement.applies_to: Standard multiselect fields (Languages, Genre, Language Description Type) that accept FK lookups

reusable_components_inventory.content: Inventory of components created during Item batch editor that can be reused

CollaboratorRolesCellEditor.purpose: Edit through-model relationships with multiple attributes (multiselect + booleans)
CollaboratorRolesCellEditor.reusability: HIGH - can be adapted for any through model with attributes

interface_pattern.props: cell (with value: array of objects), onCommit(newValue, text), onCancel, metadata (role choices)
interface_pattern.value_format: [{id, name, roles: string[], citation_author: boolean, isValid?: boolean}]
interface_pattern.text_format: Formatted string for display: 'Name (Role1, Role2; in citation)'

features.search_and_add: Autocomplete to search and add items by name
features.attribute_editing: Per-item editing: multiselect dropdown for roles, checkbox for citation
features.invalid_handling: Red cards for id: null items, block commit if present
features.change_detection: Comprehensive comparison of valid AND invalid items

for_new_through_model: 1. Update value interface (replace 'roles' and 'citation_author' with your attributes)
2. Update search API call (replace collaboratorsAPI with your API)
3. Update attribute editors (replace roles dropdown and citation checkbox with your fields)
4. Update formatting in handleCommit (generate appropriate display text)
5. Update metadata prop (pass your choice options)

TitleWithLanguageCellEditor.purpose: Edit composite field: text + FK relationship
TitleWithLanguageCellEditor.reusability: HIGH - any text + related model pattern

interface_pattern.props: cell (with value: {title: string, language: object | null}), onCommit, onCancel, metadata (languoid options)

features.fk_selection: Autocomplete with search/filter for related model

for_new_text_fk_pattern: 1. Update value interface (rename 'title' and 'language' to your field names)
2. Update Autocomplete options (replace languoidOptions with your options)

CellEditor_multiselect_enhancements.purpose: Enhanced multiselect case with invalid item handling
CellEditor_multiselect_enhancements.reusability: UNIVERSAL - applies to all multiselect fields
enhancements: Filter out id: null items on initialization
Render invalid chips with red styling (conditional based on isValid flag)
Cancel commit if invalid chips remain
Detect removal of invalid chips as change
Comprehensive valid vs invalid comparison logic

code_sections.initialization: Lines filtering invalid items or creating isValid flags
code_sections.visualization: Chip component with conditional sx styling

import_parsers.purpose: Parse complex multi-attribute fields from spreadsheet text
import_parsers.reusability: TEMPLATES - adapt patterns for similar structures

parseCollaboratorsWithRoles.output: {id, name, roles: string[], citation_author: boolean, isValid: boolean}
parseCollaboratorsWithRoles.features: Name lookup (exact + without nickname), roles parsing (map labels → values), citation parsing, uniqueness validation

parseTitleWithLanguage.features: Regex to extract text and parenthetical, language lookup by name, uniqueness validation

parseCommaSeparatedLanguoids_enhanced.features: Multiple format support, glottocode-first lookup, name fallback, uniqueness validation, PRESERVES invalid with id: null
parseCommaSeparatedLanguoids_enhanced.enhancement: Invalid preservation pattern - doesn't drop bad data

item_specific_patterns.content: Patterns specific to Item batch editor that Document likely won't need but are documented for reference

dynamic_title_columns_in_export.problem: Items can have unlimited non-default titles, but spreadsheet needs fixed columns
dynamic_title_columns_in_export.solution: Dynamic column generation: analyze max titles across all items (cap at 10), always show 'First Additional Title' even if empty, generate 'Second', 'Third', etc. as needed
dynamic_title_columns_in_export.implementation: In generate_item_export_task: iterate items, count non-default titles, determine max, generate column headers, pad row data with empty strings
dynamic_title_columns_in_export.reason: Document model unlikely to have similar unlimited-title requirement

itemtitle_through_model_default_flag.problem: Items have one 'default' title and multiple 'additional' titles
itemtitle_through_model_default_flag.solution: ItemTitle through model with default boolean, filter by default=True for primary, default=False for additional
itemtitle_through_model_default_flag.backend_handling: When updating primary_title, set old default to False, new to True. Only one default=True per item.
itemtitle_through_model_default_flag.reason: Pattern is specific to Item's title requirements

catalog_number_uniqueness_validation.problem: Catalog numbers must be unique across all items AND within current batch editor rows
catalog_number_uniqueness_validation.solution: Client-side validation that checks against cached items AND draft rows, mark cell invalid if duplicate found (but allow current row's original value)
catalog_number_uniqueness_validation.implementation: In handleCellChange: get all catalog numbers from rows + cache, check if newValue exists elsewhere, set validationState: 'invalid' if duplicate
catalog_number_uniqueness_validation.note: Document might have similar unique identifier field requiring same validation pattern

61_fields_management.challenge: Item model has 61 fields, largest batch editor to date
lessons: Column width management critical for usability
Field grouping mental model helps (dates together, geographic together, etc.)
Choice constants must be imported from centralized location (api.ts)
Multiselect and select fields need comprehensive choice mappings for import/export
61_fields_management.note: Document might be similarly large, same management strategies apply

performance_patterns_confirmed.content: Performance patterns tested and confirmed at scale with Item batch editor

redis_caching_mandatory.pattern: Backend: Redis cache with TTL, async rebuild on stale. Frontend: ItemCacheContext with getItems(forceRefresh).
redis_caching_mandatory.performance: Cache hit: <1 second. Cache rebuild: ~15-20 seconds background. Initial load: ~15-20 seconds one-time.
redis_caching_mandatory.requirement: Mandatory for any model with >1000 rows or >5MB cached payload

async_cache_rebuild.pattern: list() returns 202 Accepted if cache stale, triggers Celery task, frontend polls /items/?
async_cache_rebuild.user_experience: Dialog shows 'Cache loading, please wait...', auto-proceeds when ready
async_cache_rebuild.anti_pattern: Synchronous rebuild blocks request for 15-20 seconds, times out, bad UX

case_insensitive_sorting.problem: Default Django sorting is case-sensitive: 'Zebra' comes before 'apple'
case_insensitive_sorting.solution: Use Lower('field_name') in order_by() for queryset and tasks

async_export_threshold.pattern: >100 rows triggers async export with Celery task
async_export_threshold.timestamp_frontend: Generate YYYY-MM-DD_HHMMSS on download for human-readable filename
async_export_threshold.polling: Frontend polls /export-status/{export_id}/ every 2 seconds, 60 second timeout

client_side_filtering_full_dataset.problem: Paginated state only has 25 items, filters need full dataset
client_side_filtering_full_dataset.solution: await getItems() returns full cached dataset, apply filters client-side, send filtered IDs to backend
client_side_filtering_full_dataset.applies_to: Exports, batch edit, any operation on 'filtered' items
client_side_filtering_full_dataset.implementation: const allCachedItems = await getItems(); const filtered = applyFiltersToCache(allCachedItems); const ids = filtered.map(i => i.id);

prefetch_related_optimization.pattern: Use prefetch_related for M2M and reverse FK lookups to avoid N+1 queries
prefetch_related_optimization.impact: Reduces export time from minutes to seconds for 4,400 items

import_export_pattern_refinements.content: Refined understanding of import/export patterns from Item implementation

export_format_human_readable.principle: Export must be human-readable and editable in spreadsheet software

examples.select_fields: Export: '3 - Access protected by a time limit' (not '3')
examples.through_model_fields: Export: 'John Doe (Author, Speaker; in citation)' (not JSON)

export_format_human_readable.implementation: Use dict(CHOICES).get(value, value) to map values to labels

import_parsing_human_readable.principle: Import must accept the same human-readable format that export produces

requirements.reverse_mapping: Parser must map labels back to values using choice constants
requirements.format_flexibility: Accept multiple reasonable formats (glottocode, name, or both)
requirements.error_preservation: Don't drop invalid data, preserve with id: null for user correction

import_parsing_human_readable.implementation: Dedicated parsers in itemImportValueParsers.ts for each complex field type

parser_level_validation.principle: Validation happens at parser level for complex fields, not backend API
parser_level_validation.reasoning: Backend API can't validate virtual fields (SerializerMethodField) or complex multi-attribute fields
parser_level_validation.pattern: Parser returns {value, text, error} tuple. If error, mark cell invalid. Frontend blocks save if invalid cells.
parser_level_validation.skip_backend_validation: Add field names to skipValidationFields array in useImport hook

comparison_logic_complex_objects.problem: Simple === comparison doesn't work for objects or arrays
comparison_logic_complex_objects.solution: Custom areCellValuesEqual function with type-specific comparison logic

examples.collaborator_arrays: Compare sorted arrays of {id, roles[], citation_author}

comparison_logic_complex_objects.purpose: Prevent imported data that matches existing data from showing as yellow (changed)

new_row_identification.pattern: New rows have IDs starting with 'new-{timestamp}' prefix
new_row_identification.validation_strategy: Skip backend validation for new rows (isNewRow check), only validate parsers
new_row_identification.cell_type_mapping: Use correct cellType when creating draft rows (not hardcoded 'text')

debugging_lessons.content: Debugging strategies discovered during Item implementation, especially async export troubleshooting

celery_silent_failures.symptom: Celery logs show task received but no execution logs, task marked complete, file corrupted or wrong size
celery_silent_failures.root_cause: Task crashes before first line of code executes (decorator issue, import error, function signature mismatch)
debugging_strategy: Add logging BEFORE try block to prove task starts
Check decorator parameters match function signature
Verify all imports at top of function (not just in try block)
Compare with working task from same file
Check for syntax errors: python3 -m py_compile tasks.py
celery_silent_failures.lesson_learned: Task execution failures can be silent. Need aggressive early logging to detect.

cached_file_detection.symptom: Export 'succeeds' and file size looks correct, but user gets old data
cached_file_detection.root_cause: Celery task fails, but old file from previous run still exists with same filename
debugging_strategy: Check file timestamp: ls -lh media/exports/ | grep {export_id}
Compare file timestamp to current test time
Check if export_id is reused (timestamp collision)
Verify task actually executed (check for task logs)
cached_file_detection.solution: Use UUID for export_id to guarantee uniqueness, prevents stale file collisions

uuid_vs_timestamp_tradeoffs.uuid_benefits: Guaranteed uniqueness, no timezone issues, no collision risk
uuid_vs_timestamp_tradeoffs.uuid_drawbacks: Not human-readable, not sortable by time
uuid_vs_timestamp_tradeoffs.timestamp_benefits: Human-readable, sortable, indicates when export created
uuid_vs_timestamp_tradeoffs.timestamp_drawbacks: Collision risk, timezone complexity, parsing issues
uuid_vs_timestamp_tradeoffs.hybrid_solution: UUID on backend (uniqueness), timestamp on frontend (readability)

client_side_filtering_verification.symptom: Export sends 0 items or wrong number of items
client_side_filtering_verification.root_cause: Using paginated state instead of full cached dataset
debugging_strategy: Add console.log for activeFilterCount, activeFilters object, IDs array length
Verify getItems() returns full dataset (not paginated slice)
Check if filters correctly map to Item interface properties
client_side_filtering_verification.lesson_learned: Always log the IDs being sent to verify correct filtering

cache_invalidation_unwanted_rebuilds.root_cause: Post-save signal invalidates cache, triggering async rebuild
cache_invalidation_unwanted_rebuilds.solution: Set instance._skip_async_tasks = True in save_batch() to prevent signal from invalidating cache
cache_invalidation_unwanted_rebuilds.lesson_learned: Bulk operations need to suppress cache invalidation, update cache manually

typescript_property_name_mismatches.symptom: Filters don't work, comparison fails, undefined property access
typescript_property_name_mismatches.root_cause: Frontend Item interface property names don't match actual API response
typescript_property_name_mismatches.example: Using item.description_scope_and_content when interface defines item.description
debugging_strategy: Check actual API response in Network tab
grep for all usages of property to find mismatches
Use TypeScript strict mode to catch undefined access
typescript_property_name_mismatches.lesson_learned: Keep Item interface in sync with backend serializer field names

document_batch_editor_preparation_checkl.content: Comprehensive checklist for efficient Document batch editor implementation
pre_implementation_reading: ☐ Read THIS FILE (item-batch-editor-lessons.json) for latest state-of-the-art
model_analysis: ☐ List all Document model fields (how many?)
☐ Identify complex fields requiring custom editors (through models? virtual fields?)
☐ Identify fields with choice constants (create/verify in api.ts)
☐ Identify unique fields requiring validation (like catalog_number)
☐ Check for M2M relationships (will need multiselect)
☐ Check for through models with attributes (need custom editor)
☐ Check for SerializerMethodField virtual columns (skip backend validation)
☐ Estimate cache size: row count × field count × avg field size
architecture_planning: ☐ Plan Redis caching from start (don't retrofit)
☐ Plan async export from start (UUID backend, timestamp frontend)
☐ Plan client-side filtering using full cache (not paginated state)
☐ Identify reusable components from Item (CollaboratorRolesCellEditor? TitleWithLanguageCellEditor?)
☐ Plan custom editors needed (adapt existing or create new?)
☐ Plan import parsers needed (adapt existing patterns)
☐ List fields to skip backend validation (virtual fields, through models with attributes)
implementation_phase_1_backend: ☐ Implement update/create logic for through models
☐ Implement Redis caching in list() method (check cache, 202 if stale)
☐ Create generate_document_export_task Celery task (UUID + return error dict)
implementation_phase_2_frontend_basic: ☐ Update DocumentsList.tsx for batch edit and async export
☐ Define column configs with correct cellTypes
☐ Implement handleCellChange with validation for standard fields
implementation_phase_3_complex_fields: ☐ Identify which custom editors to reuse (Collaborator? Title? Both? Neither?)
☐ Adapt or create custom editors for Document-specific through models
☐ Add cellType cases to CellEditor.tsx if needed
☐ Add serialization cases to TanStackSpreadsheet.tsx for structured copy/paste
☐ Implement invalid data preservation (id: null) for FK lookups
☐ Implement red chip/card visualization for invalid items
☐ Test commit/cancel logic for complex editors
implementation_phase_4_import_export: ☐ Create documentImportValueParsers.ts (adapt Item parsers as templates)
☐ Implement parsers for complex fields (through models, text+FK, etc.)
☐ Test import with exported spreadsheet (round-trip test)
☐ Test import with invalid data (verify preservation and visualization)
implementation_phase_5_testing_and_refin: ☐ Test with small dataset (10 rows) - verify all field types work
☐ Test with medium dataset (100 rows) - verify async export triggers
☐ Test with large dataset (1000+ rows) - verify Redis cache performance
☐ Test new row creation and persistence across browser refresh
☐ Test validation for unique fields (if any)
☐ Test invalid data import and user correction workflow
☐ Test all 6 universal patterns are working correctly
☐ Compare with Item batch editor - any patterns missed?
☐ Update contextual memory with Document-specific lessons learned
efficiency_tips: Copy Item batch editor files as starting templates (don't start from scratch)
Use Item import parsers as templates (don't rewrite logic)
Reference Item serializer for SerializerMethodField and through-model patterns
Use same Redis caching pattern (don't reinvent)
Use same async export pattern with UUID (proven to work)
Test incrementally - don't implement everything before first test

anti_patterns_summary.content: Summary of anti-patterns to avoid based on Item implementation iterations

anti_patterns.why_bad: Will hit sessionStorage quota, requires painful retrofit
anti_patterns.correct: Implement Redis caching from first iteration

anti_patterns.pattern: Using timestamp for export_id in backend
anti_patterns.why_bad: Collision risk, timezone issues, subtle bugs
anti_patterns.correct: Use UUID in backend, format timestamp on frontend download

anti_patterns.pattern: Using paginated state for filtered operations
anti_patterns.why_bad: Only operates on 25 rows instead of full filtered dataset
anti_patterns.correct: Use full cached dataset, apply filters client-side

anti_patterns.why_bad: User loses data, doesn't know why, can't fix it
anti_patterns.correct: Preserve invalid data with id: null, show red, allow correction

anti_patterns.pattern: Validating virtual fields via backend API
anti_patterns.why_bad: Backend can't validate SerializerMethodField, returns 405 error
anti_patterns.correct: Skip backend validation for virtual fields, validate in parser

anti_patterns.pattern: Not updating sessionStorage config after save
anti_patterns.why_bad: New rows disappear on browser refresh
anti_patterns.correct: Update sessionStorage config with new row IDs after successful save

anti_patterns.pattern: Using raise self.retry() in Celery tasks without understanding
anti_patterns.why_bad: Can cause silent failures and corrupted files
anti_patterns.correct: Use return {'status': 'error'} pattern for clearer error handling

anti_patterns.pattern: Hardcoding choice constants in batch editor files
anti_patterns.why_bad: Duplicated data, goes out of sync with backend, maintenance nightmare
anti_patterns.correct: Import choice constants from centralized api.ts

anti_patterns.pattern: Not using Lower() for text field sorting
anti_patterns.why_bad: Case-sensitive sort: 'Zebra' before 'apple' is confusing to users

anti_patterns.pattern: Forgetting prefetch_related for M2M and reverse FK
anti_patterns.why_bad: N+1 query problem, exports take minutes instead of seconds
anti_patterns.correct: Always prefetch_related for related data in list/export queries

success_metrics.content: Metrics demonstrating Item batch editor success and readiness as reference implementation

pattern_adherence.redis_caching: Yes (retrofitted, should be from start next time)

user_experience.save_time: <2 seconds for batch of changed rows
user_experience.error_handling: Red cells with tooltips, preserves user input

readiness_for_document.confidence_level: High - ready for efficient Document implementation

filter_persistence_and_client_side_filte.content: Critical lessons about filter persistence and client-side filtering parity with backend
filter_persistence_and_client_side_filte.conversation_reference: Debugging batch edit filtered and export filtered discrepancies (697 vs 685 items)

problem_discovered.symptom: Item list page showed 697 items with 'ass' keyword filter, but batch editor and export only loaded 685 items
problem_discovered.root_cause: Client-side filter logic in applyFiltersToCache didn't have access to all fields needed to match backend's filter_keyword behavior
problem_discovered.specific_issue: Backend's keyword filter searches titles via title_item__title__icontains JOIN, but InternalItemBatchSerializer didn't include titles field - only primary_title and secondary_title

solution_implemented.backend_change: Added titles SerializerMethodField to InternalItemBatchSerializer to return all ItemTitle objects with {id, title, language, language_name, language_iso, default}
solution_implemented.rationale: Client-side filtering requires same data that backend filtering uses. Without titles array, frontend couldn't replicate backend's title searching

field_name_mismatch_discovered.problem: Frontend filter checked item.description but backend uses description_scope_and_content
field_name_mismatch_discovered.impact: All keyword searches involving descriptions failed because field didn't exist in cached data
field_name_mismatch_discovered.fix: Updated frontend filter to use item.description_scope_and_content and added to Item TypeScript interface
field_name_mismatch_discovered.lesson: Always verify field names match between frontend TypeScript interfaces and backend serializer output

collaborators_field_structure_correction.problem: Frontend checked collab.collaborator_data.full_name but batch serializer returns collab.name
collaborators_field_structure_correction.impact: Collaborator name filtering was broken in client-side filter
collaborators_field_structure_correction.fix: Updated frontend to check collab.name and added proper collaborators type to Item interface
collaborators_field_structure_correction.lesson: Batch serializers may have different structure than full serializers - always check actual serializer output

filter_persistence_pattern.requirement: Item list filters should persist across navigation like Collaborator and Languoid lists
key_elements: clearPersistedFilters function for clearing both state and storage

client_side_filter_parity_requirements.critical_rule: Client-side applyFiltersToCache MUST check the same fields that backend's filter methods check
client_side_filter_parity_requirements.ensuring_parity: If batch serializer doesn't include necessary fields for filtering, ADD THEM. Don't compromise filter accuracy.

list_page_ui_consistency.requirement: Item list page UI should match Collaborator/Languoid pattern
changes_made: Page title changed from 'Items ({count})' to just 'Items'
Added results count below filters: '{count} item(s) found'
Matches pattern used in CollaboratorsList.tsx line 1050-1051
list_page_ui_consistency.benefits: Consistent UX across all list pages, cleaner header section

navigation_menu_ordering.change: Collections moved before Items in navigation menu
navigation_menu_ordering.rationale: Reflects hierarchical relationship (Items belong to Collections)

effective_strategy: 1. Add logging to both frontend (console.log) and backend (logger.info) to compare counts
2. Log sample item structure from cache to verify field availability
3. Add detailed filter match logging (catalogMatch, descMatch, titlesMatch, etc.)
4. Compare frontend filter logic line-by-line with backend filter_keyword method
5. Verify TypeScript interfaces match actual API response structure
tools_used: Backend logs for Celery task execution
Network tab to inspect API response structure
Comparison of serializer output vs TypeScript interface

checklist: ✅ Ensure InternalDocumentBatchSerializer includes ALL fields needed for client-side filtering
✅ Verify TypeScript Document interface matches serializer output exactly
✅ Implement filter persistence using usePersistedListState from the start
✅ Follow Item list page UI pattern (title only, count below filters)
✅ Test that backend filter count matches frontend applyFiltersToCache count
✅ Add comprehensive logging during initial implementation for easier debugging
